title: ITSI Glass Table NOC Display Render Latency, Widget Refresh Stability, Live-Connection Throughput, and Big-Screen TV Mode Reliability

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: required — Glass Tables, Service Analyzer, KPI searches bound to widgets

dataSources: index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_web_access uri_path=*/glass_tables/* for glass_table_id, user, useragent, referer, bytes, status, response_time_ms, app, splunk_server; index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler savedsearch_id patterns for glass table widget KPI refresh with result_count and status; index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd ERROR narratives mentioning glass table or widget render failures; index=_audit actions referencing /glass_tables/ for kiosk session and edit telemetry; lookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv for display_name, owner_team, noc_tier, slo_render_p95_target_ms, slo_success_pct_target, kiosk_session_warn_min, is_noc_wall; optional accelerated datamodel=Performance for tstats anchor on search head hosts
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.8 ITSI Glass Table NOC render latency widget refresh live connection TV kiosk multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_web_access earliest=-24h@h latest=now uri_path=*/glass_tables/*
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval uri_path=trim(toString(coalesce(uri_path,"")))
      | rex field=uri_path "/glass_tables/(?<glass_table_id>[^/\?#]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__missing_gt__"))))
      | eval response_time_ms=tonumber(coalesce(response_time_ms,req_time_ms,request_time_ms,elapsed_ms,"0"))
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(bytes,0))
      | eval status=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status,200))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user,user_name,"")))
      | eval useragent=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(useragent,http_user_agent,""))),1,256)
      | eval referer=trim(toString(coalesce(referer,referrer,"")))
      | eval app=trim(toString(coalesce(app,"itsi")))
      | eval widget_id="__page__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval lane="web_access"
      | eval live_drop=if(status==499 OR status==0 OR match(_raw,"client.?abort|connection.?reset|ECONNRESET"),1,0)
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=if(status>=500 OR response_time_ms>10000,1,0)
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-24h@h latest=now (savedsearch_id=*glass_table* OR savedsearch_id=*glass* OR match(lower(savedsearch_id),"glasstable") OR match(lower(savedsearch_id),"itsi.*kpi.*glass"))
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,savedsearch_name,"__sched_unknown__"))))
      | rex field=savedsearch_id "(?i)glass[_\-\s]*table[_\-\s]*(?<glass_table_id>[a-z0-9\.\-\_]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__sched_unknown__"))))
      | eval widget_id=trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,"__widget__")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"__kpi__")))
      | eval service_id=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,serviceid,"__svc__")))
      | eval uri_path=strcat("/scheduler/",savedsearch_id)
      | eval useragent="scheduler"
      | eval referer=splunk_server
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user,"")))
      | eval app="itsi"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(result_count,scan_count,0))
      | eval response_time_ms=coalesce(tonumber(run_time_ms),tonumber(run_time)*1000,0)
      | eval status=if(match(lower(tostring(status)),"success"),200,500)
      | eval lane="sched_widget"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval widget_fail=if(match(lower(tostring(status)),"success")==0 OR tonumber(coalesce(result_count,1))<1 OR match(lower(tostring(status)),"skip|deferred|continued"),1,0)
      | eval render_exc=0
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h@h latest=now (log_level=ERROR OR match(lower(_raw),"error"))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"glass|glasstable|glass_table|widget")
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)glass[_\s\-]*table[_\s\-]*(?<glass_table_id>[a-z0-9\.\-\_]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__err_parse__"))))
      | eval uri_path="splunkd_error"
      | eval user="splunkd"
      | eval useragent="splunkd"
      | eval referer=""
      | eval app="itsi"
      | eval widget_id="__exception__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval bytes=0
      | eval response_time_ms=0
      | eval status=500
      | eval lane="splunkd_err"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=1
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"glass|/glass_tables/")
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,dnt,""))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user,"")))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "/glass_tables/(?<glass_table_id>[^\"\s\?]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__audit_unknown__"))))
      | eval uri_path=trim(toString(coalesce(object,"/glass_tables/")))
      | eval useragent="audit"
      | eval referer=trim(toString(coalesce(action,operation,"")))
      | eval app="itsi"
      | eval widget_id="__audit__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval bytes=0
      | eval response_time_ms=0
      | eval status=200
      | eval lane="audit_action"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval kiosk_evt=if(match(lr,"login|logout|session|password|expired|timeout|authentication"),1,0)
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=0 ]
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS perf_anchor FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS splunk_server
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(splunk_server)))
      | eval glass_table_id="__perf_anchor__"
      | eval uri_path="/perf/datamodel"
      | eval user="__tstats__"
      | eval useragent="tstats"
      | eval referer="Performance"
      | eval app="platform"
      | eval widget_id="__tstats__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval bytes=0
      | eval response_time_ms=0
      | eval status=200
      | eval lane="tstats_perf"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=0
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
| eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__unknown__"))))
| eval kiosk_evt=coalesce(kiosk_evt,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    perc95(eval(if(lane=="web_access",response_time_ms,null()))) AS render_p95_ms
    sum(eval(if(lane=="web_access",1,0))) AS web_hits
    sum(eval(if(lane=="web_access",live_drop,0))) AS live_drop_hits
    sum(widget_fail) AS widget_fail_hits
    sum(render_exc) AS render_exc_hits
    sum(kiosk_evt) AS kiosk_evt_hits
    dc(eval(if(lane=="web_access",user,null()))) AS dc_viewers
    values(eval(if(lane=="web_access",user,null()))) AS user_vals
    sum(eval(if(lane=="audit_action",1,0))) AS audit_views
    values(uri_path) AS uri_path
    values(app) AS app
    max(bytes) AS bytes
    max(status) AS status
    values(widget_id) AS widget_id
    values(kpi_id) AS kpi_id
    values(service_id) AS service_id
    max(_time) AS last_seen
  BY _time splunk_server glass_table_id
| eval user=mvindex(mvdedup(user_vals),0)
| fields - user_vals
| eval live_drop_pct=if(web_hits>0,round(100*live_drop_hits/web_hits,2),0)
| sort 0 glass_table_id splunk_server + _time
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f
    avg(render_p95_ms) AS render_p95_ma
    sum(web_hits) AS rolling_web_hits
  BY glass_table_id splunk_server
| eventstats median(render_p95_ms) AS sh_render_med BY splunk_server
| join type=left max=0 glass_table_id
    [| inputlookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv
     | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,gt_id,""))))
     | eval display_name=trim(toString(coalesce(display_name,title,"")))
     | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")))
     | eval noc_tier=trim(toString(coalesce(noc_tier,"standard")))
     | eval slo_render_p95_target_ms=tonumber(coalesce(slo_render_p95_target_ms,5000))
     | eval slo_success_pct_target=tonumber(coalesce(slo_success_pct_target,95))
     | eval kiosk_session_warn_min=tonumber(coalesce(kiosk_session_warn_min,480))
     | eval is_noc_wall=tonumber(coalesce(is_noc_wall,0))
     | fields glass_table_id display_name owner_team noc_tier slo_render_p95_target_ms slo_success_pct_target kiosk_session_warn_min is_noc_wall ]
| eval display_name=coalesce(display_name,"unregistered_table")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval noc_tier=coalesce(noc_tier,"standard")
| eval slo_render_p95_target_ms=coalesce(slo_render_p95_target_ms,5000)
| eval slo_success_pct_target=coalesce(slo_success_pct_target,95)
| eval kiosk_session_warn_min=coalesce(kiosk_session_warn_min,480)
| eval slo_latency_met=if(render_p95_ms<=slo_render_p95_target_ms AND coalesce(render_exc_hits,0)<1,1,0)
| eventstats
    sum(eval(if(is_noc_wall==1 AND slo_latency_met==1,1,0))) AS noc_ok_cnt
    sum(eval(if(is_noc_wall==1,1,0))) AS noc_wall_cnt
  BY _time splunk_server
| eval noc_slo_success_pct=if(noc_wall_cnt>0,round(100*noc_ok_cnt/noc_wall_cnt,2),null())
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(render_p95_ms,0)>10000 OR coalesce(render_exc_hits,0)>=5 OR coalesce(live_drop_pct,0)>35,"critical",
    coalesce(render_p95_ms,0)>coalesce(slo_render_p95_target_ms,5000) OR coalesce(widget_fail_hits,0)>=12 OR coalesce(kiosk_evt_hits,0)>=8,"high",
    coalesce(render_p95_ms,0)>5000 OR coalesce(live_drop_pct,0)>15 OR coalesce(widget_fail_hits,0)>=5,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(live_drop_pct,0)>20,"inspect_reverse_proxy_websocket_sse_keepalives_and_search_head_load_for_glass_table_sessions",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(render_exc_hits,0)>=1,"tail_splunk_web_service_and_splunkd_on_sh_members_clear_js_bundle_cache_retest_glass_table_editor",
    severity=="high" AND coalesce(kiosk_evt_hits,0)>=3,"extend_splunk_session_timeout_for_kiosk_tv_accounts_review_web_conf_sso_idle_policies",
    severity=="high" AND coalesce(widget_fail_hits,0)>=5,"drill_scheduler_failures_for_widget_kpi_saved_searches_validate_itsi_kpi_base_search_freshness",
    severity=="medium","reduce_widget_count_enable_summary_indexes_stagger_auto_refresh_intervals_on_dense_glass_tables",
    true(),"continue_weekly_noc_glass_table_slo_review_and_prune_orphan_views")
| table glass_table_id display_name splunk_server owner_team noc_tier uri_path user useragent referer bytes status app widget_id kpi_id service_id render_p95_ms render_p95_ma web_hits dc_viewers audit_views live_drop_pct widget_fail_hits kiosk_evt_hits noc_slo_success_pct sh_render_med severity recommended_action last_seen
description: Detects Splunk ITSI Glass Table NOC wall failures by correlating Splunk Web /glass_tables/ access render latency P95, per-table viewer counts, scheduler widget refresh failures, splunkd render exceptions, audit adoption signals, live-connection drop rates, kiosk-style authentication churn hints, and fleet NOC SLO coverage using inventory targets joined from itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv.
value: Operations leaders keep twenty-four-seven wall displays trustworthy during incidents by proving which glass tables actually paint under five seconds, which widgets silently stop refreshing while neighbors look green, when live browser channels drop behind proxies, and which displays are orphaned configuration debt versus actively watched NOC assets.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the big status boards on the operations center wall the same way you would watch a train departures screen. When the board takes too long to refresh, drops its live feed, shows blank tiles, or forces a login during an emergency, we surface which board misbehaved and who should fix it.
implementation: Schedule uc_13_2_8_itsi_glass_table_noc_reliability every fifteen minutes on the ITSI search heads with earliest=-24h@h; forward _internal splunk_web_access and splunkd error lines, scheduler completions, and _audit; maintain lookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv; tune severity gates per tenant; page on critical or high with thirty-minute dedupe; archive weekly CSV exports for NOC governance.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with Splunk IT Service Intelligence administrators who curate Glass Table layouts for network operations centers, reliability engineers who size search head clusters driving dense widget grids, and workplace technology teams that operate kiosk browsers on large-format displays. This use case isolates the presentation and refresh plane for ITSI Glass Tables: the Splunk Web requests that hydrate a glass table document, the scheduled searches that repaint individual KPI tiles, the Splunk Web and splunkd error paths that surface JavaScript or rendering failures, the audit trail that proves which identities touched which glass table objects, and the fleet-level service level objective that states what fraction of wall-mounted NOC glass tables must paint faster than five seconds at the ninety-fifth percentile. UC-13.2.1 remains historical Service Health Score trending without judging whether the Glass Table canvas itself stalled. UC-13.2.2 remains KPI degradation alerting on breach semantics rather than per-widget scheduler success inside a single glass table composition. UC-13.2.3 remains episode volume and mean time to resolve analytics. UC-13.2.5 remains KPI base search runtime economics and pseudo-entity discovery for shared bases; this document consumes those signals only when widget-level refresh failures cluster on a specific glass table identity. UC-13.2.6 remains Event Analytics rules engine fidelity. UC-13.2.7 remains predictive service health forecast quality. UC-13.2.18 remains adaptive threshold calibration. None of those siblings answer whether a fifty-widget NOC wall froze for twelve seconds, whether live push channels dropped mid-incident, or whether kiosk sessions expired during a sev-one bridge.

Glass Tables combine Simple XML or Dashboard Studio style panels with ITSI service and KPI bindings, background images, and high widget counts that stress browser layout, JavaScript bundle parsing, and concurrent search fan-out. Each glass table has a stable identifier that appears in REST paths under /servicesNS or in Splunk Web access logs as uri_path segments matching /glass_tables/<id>. Operators on a TV often run kiosk full-screen profiles with long idle timeouts; platform defaults for session TTL, reverse-proxy idle timers, and single sign-on refresh still force re-authentication if WebSocket or Server-Sent Event channels fall behind. Live updates depend on healthy search heads, captain stability on clusters, and sufficient concurrency for auto-refresh schedules tied to each widget.

Role-based access to index=_internal, index=_audit, and the itsi application requires sober service accounts for automation searches. Legal reviewers may care that access logs retain user and referer fields; mask identities in executive exports when policy demands. Forwarders must ship splunk_web_access and splunkd error channels from every search head cluster member because browsers load-balance across nodes.

Index design: keep at least thirty days of _internal web access for quarterly NOC reviews, align _audit retention with security baselines, and document lookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv in version control with columns glass_table_id, display_name, owner_team, noc_tier, slo_render_p95_target_ms, slo_success_pct_target, kiosk_session_warn_min, and is_noc_wall for cross-team SLO math.

Risk briefing: synthetic robots that crawl dashboards can inflate hit counts without representing human NOC usage; tag bot useragent strings in a side lookup before interpreting dc_viewers. Content-pack imports can rename glass table identifiers while old URLs linger in bookmarks, producing duplicate rows until inventory is reconciled. Maintenance windows that pause KPI bases create widget_fail_hits spikes that look like Glass Table failures but originate upstream; correlate with UC-13.2.5 before blaming the canvas.

Differentiation recap: Glass Table NOC display reliability, render latency percentiles, widget refresh stability, live connection drop rates, kiosk authentication churn, orphan versus wall-registered inventory, and fleet SLO coverage for is_noc_wall tables—not composite health score curves alone, not generic KPI breach counts without presentation context, not episode analytics, not predictive residuals, not adaptive threshold drift.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Forward splunk_web_access lines from $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/ into index=_internal with sourcetype=splunk_web_access on every search head that serves Splunk Web. Confirm uri_path captures /glass_tables/ segments after ITSI enables the glass table editor. Preserve user, referer, useragent, bytes, status, and elapsed or request timing fields your platform maps into response_time_ms; if only raw durations exist, normalize with props.conf FIELDALIAS.

Schedule metadata exports or scripted inputs that refresh lookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv hourly from ITSI REST /servicesNS/nobody/SA-ITOA/storage/collections or from configuration management snapshots of glass table JSON. Mark is_noc_wall=1 only after facilities and ops leadership bless a physical display; everything else stays inventory for orphan detection.

Enable index=_audit collection for object access pertinent to glass tables, including saves and views under the ITSI app namespace. Trim noise with transforms if needed but retain authentication and timeout-adjacent actions for kiosk analysis.

Ship splunkd ERROR lines that mention glass table, widget, or visualization failures to index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd. Increase TRUNCATE if stack traces exceed defaults.

Scheduler lane: retain index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler with savedsearch_id patterns that ITSI uses for KPI searches backing glass widgets. Typical tenants include substrings such as glass_table, itsi, kpi, and glass; tune match clauses without blanketing all scheduled searches.

Performance datamodel acceleration: accelerate at least the Performance.Performance root on hosts that represent search heads so the tstats anchor arm proves datamodel health in the same job as glass table telemetry.

props.conf on heavy forwarders should not strip query strings from uri_path when glass_table_id appears in path segments only.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_8_itsi_glass_table_noc_reliability with a fifteen-minute schedule, dispatch earliest=-24h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate glass_table_id and splunk_server pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include owner_team, noc_tier, recommended_action, render_p95_ms, live_drop_pct, and widget_fail_hits in pager bodies with deep links to the Glass Table editor and Service Analyzer for bound services.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Splunk Web access for /glass_tables/, scheduler outcomes for widget-bearing saved searches, splunkd error lines referencing glass table rendering, _audit actions for glass table objects, and a Performance datamodel tstats anchor so silent acceleration does not hide a broken search head. stats collapses fifteen-minute bins per glass_table_id and splunk_server, computing render_p95_ms from web lanes only, live_drop_pct from aborted responses, widget_fail_hits from non-success scheduler rows, render_exc_hits from error logs, kiosk_evt_hits from authentication-adjacent audit strings, dc_viewers and audit_views for adoption analytics. streamstats smooths short spikes in render_p95_ma. eventstats adds search-head median context and fleet NOC SLO rollups for is_noc_wall tables. join enriches governance fields from itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv using an inputlookup subsearch. case maps severity; recommended_action encodes runbook hints for proxies, bundles, kiosk timeouts, and KPI scheduler follow-through.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.8 ITSI Glass Table NOC render latency widget refresh live connection TV kiosk multisearch earliest=-24h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_web_access earliest=-24h@h latest=now uri_path=*/glass_tables/*
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval uri_path=trim(toString(coalesce(uri_path,"")))
      | rex field=uri_path "/glass_tables/(?<glass_table_id>[^/\?#]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__missing_gt__"))))
      | eval response_time_ms=tonumber(coalesce(response_time_ms,req_time_ms,request_time_ms,elapsed_ms,"0"))
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(bytes,0))
      | eval status=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status,200))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user,user_name,"")))
      | eval useragent=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(useragent,http_user_agent,""))),1,256)
      | eval referer=trim(toString(coalesce(referer,referrer,"")))
      | eval app=trim(toString(coalesce(app,"itsi")))
      | eval widget_id="__page__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval lane="web_access"
      | eval live_drop=if(status==499 OR status==0 OR match(_raw,"client.?abort|connection.?reset|ECONNRESET"),1,0)
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=if(status>=500 OR response_time_ms>10000,1,0)
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-24h@h latest=now (savedsearch_id=*glass_table* OR savedsearch_id=*glass* OR match(lower(savedsearch_id),"glasstable") OR match(lower(savedsearch_id),"itsi.*kpi.*glass"))
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | eval savedsearch_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,savedsearch_name,"__sched_unknown__"))))
      | rex field=savedsearch_id "(?i)glass[_\-\s]*table[_\-\s]*(?<glass_table_id>[a-z0-9\.\-\_]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__sched_unknown__"))))
      | eval widget_id=trim(toString(coalesce(savedsearch_id,"__widget__")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"__kpi__")))
      | eval service_id=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,serviceid,"__svc__")))
      | eval uri_path=strcat("/scheduler/",savedsearch_id)
      | eval useragent="scheduler"
      | eval referer=splunk_server
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user,"")))
      | eval app="itsi"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(result_count,scan_count,0))
      | eval response_time_ms=coalesce(tonumber(run_time_ms),tonumber(run_time)*1000,0)
      | eval status=if(match(lower(tostring(status)),"success"),200,500)
      | eval lane="sched_widget"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval widget_fail=if(match(lower(tostring(status)),"success")==0 OR tonumber(coalesce(result_count,1))<1 OR match(lower(tostring(status)),"skip|deferred|continued"),1,0)
      | eval render_exc=0
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-24h@h latest=now (log_level=ERROR OR match(lower(_raw),"error"))
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"glass|glasstable|glass_table|widget")
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,""))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?i)glass[_\s\-]*table[_\s\-]*(?<glass_table_id>[a-z0-9\.\-\_]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__err_parse__"))))
      | eval uri_path="splunkd_error"
      | eval user="splunkd"
      | eval useragent="splunkd"
      | eval referer=""
      | eval app="itsi"
      | eval widget_id="__exception__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval bytes=0
      | eval response_time_ms=0
      | eval status=500
      | eval lane="splunkd_err"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=1
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
    [ search index=_audit earliest=-24h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"glass|/glass_tables/")
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_server,host,dnt,""))))
      | eval user=trim(toString(coalesce(user,"")))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "/glass_tables/(?<glass_table_id>[^\"\s\?]+)"
      | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__audit_unknown__"))))
      | eval uri_path=trim(toString(coalesce(object,"/glass_tables/")))
      | eval useragent="audit"
      | eval referer=trim(toString(coalesce(action,operation,"")))
      | eval app="itsi"
      | eval widget_id="__audit__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval bytes=0
      | eval response_time_ms=0
      | eval status=200
      | eval lane="audit_action"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval kiosk_evt=if(match(lr,"login|logout|session|password|expired|timeout|authentication"),1,0)
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=0 ]
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS perf_anchor FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS splunk_server
      | eval splunk_server=lower(trim(toString(splunk_server)))
      | eval glass_table_id="__perf_anchor__"
      | eval uri_path="/perf/datamodel"
      | eval user="__tstats__"
      | eval useragent="tstats"
      | eval referer="Performance"
      | eval app="platform"
      | eval widget_id="__tstats__"
      | eval kpi_id="__rollup__"
      | eval service_id="__rollup__"
      | eval bytes=0
      | eval response_time_ms=0
      | eval status=200
      | eval lane="tstats_perf"
      | eval live_drop=0
      | eval widget_fail=0
      | eval render_exc=0
      | eval kiosk_evt=0 ]
| eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,"__unknown__"))))
| eval kiosk_evt=coalesce(kiosk_evt,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats
    perc95(eval(if(lane=="web_access",response_time_ms,null()))) AS render_p95_ms
    sum(eval(if(lane=="web_access",1,0))) AS web_hits
    sum(eval(if(lane=="web_access",live_drop,0))) AS live_drop_hits
    sum(widget_fail) AS widget_fail_hits
    sum(render_exc) AS render_exc_hits
    sum(kiosk_evt) AS kiosk_evt_hits
    dc(eval(if(lane=="web_access",user,null()))) AS dc_viewers
    values(eval(if(lane=="web_access",user,null()))) AS user_vals
    sum(eval(if(lane=="audit_action",1,0))) AS audit_views
    values(uri_path) AS uri_path
    values(app) AS app
    max(bytes) AS bytes
    max(status) AS status
    values(widget_id) AS widget_id
    values(kpi_id) AS kpi_id
    values(service_id) AS service_id
    max(_time) AS last_seen
  BY _time splunk_server glass_table_id
| eval user=mvindex(mvdedup(user_vals),0)
| fields - user_vals
| eval live_drop_pct=if(web_hits>0,round(100*live_drop_hits/web_hits,2),0)
| sort 0 glass_table_id splunk_server + _time
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f
    avg(render_p95_ms) AS render_p95_ma
    sum(web_hits) AS rolling_web_hits
  BY glass_table_id splunk_server
| eventstats median(render_p95_ms) AS sh_render_med BY splunk_server
| join type=left max=0 glass_table_id
    [| inputlookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv
     | eval glass_table_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(glass_table_id,gt_id,""))))
     | eval display_name=trim(toString(coalesce(display_name,title,"")))
     | eval owner_team=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_team,team,"unowned")))
     | eval noc_tier=trim(toString(coalesce(noc_tier,"standard")))
     | eval slo_render_p95_target_ms=tonumber(coalesce(slo_render_p95_target_ms,5000))
     | eval slo_success_pct_target=tonumber(coalesce(slo_success_pct_target,95))
     | eval kiosk_session_warn_min=tonumber(coalesce(kiosk_session_warn_min,480))
     | eval is_noc_wall=tonumber(coalesce(is_noc_wall,0))
     | fields glass_table_id display_name owner_team noc_tier slo_render_p95_target_ms slo_success_pct_target kiosk_session_warn_min is_noc_wall ]
| eval display_name=coalesce(display_name,"unregistered_table")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval noc_tier=coalesce(noc_tier,"standard")
| eval slo_render_p95_target_ms=coalesce(slo_render_p95_target_ms,5000)
| eval slo_success_pct_target=coalesce(slo_success_pct_target,95)
| eval kiosk_session_warn_min=coalesce(kiosk_session_warn_min,480)
| eval slo_latency_met=if(render_p95_ms<=slo_render_p95_target_ms AND coalesce(render_exc_hits,0)<1,1,0)
| eventstats
    sum(eval(if(is_noc_wall==1 AND slo_latency_met==1,1,0))) AS noc_ok_cnt
    sum(eval(if(is_noc_wall==1,1,0))) AS noc_wall_cnt
  BY _time splunk_server
| eval noc_slo_success_pct=if(noc_wall_cnt>0,round(100*noc_ok_cnt/noc_wall_cnt,2),null())
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(render_p95_ms,0)>10000 OR coalesce(render_exc_hits,0)>=5 OR coalesce(live_drop_pct,0)>35,"critical",
    coalesce(render_p95_ms,0)>coalesce(slo_render_p95_target_ms,5000) OR coalesce(widget_fail_hits,0)>=12 OR coalesce(kiosk_evt_hits,0)>=8,"high",
    coalesce(render_p95_ms,0)>5000 OR coalesce(live_drop_pct,0)>15 OR coalesce(widget_fail_hits,0)>=5,"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(live_drop_pct,0)>20,"inspect_reverse_proxy_websocket_sse_keepalives_and_search_head_load_for_glass_table_sessions",
    severity=="critical" AND coalesce(render_exc_hits,0)>=1,"tail_splunk_web_service_and_splunkd_on_sh_members_clear_js_bundle_cache_retest_glass_table_editor",
    severity=="high" AND coalesce(kiosk_evt_hits,0)>=3,"extend_splunk_session_timeout_for_kiosk_tv_accounts_review_web_conf_sso_idle_policies",
    severity=="high" AND coalesce(widget_fail_hits,0)>=5,"drill_scheduler_failures_for_widget_kpi_saved_searches_validate_itsi_kpi_base_search_freshness",
    severity=="medium","reduce_widget_count_enable_summary_indexes_stagger_auto_refresh_intervals_on_dense_glass_tables",
    true(),"continue_weekly_noc_glass_table_slo_review_and_prune_orphan_views")
| table glass_table_id display_name splunk_server owner_team noc_tier uri_path user useragent referer bytes status app widget_id kpi_id service_id render_p95_ms render_p95_ma web_hits dc_viewers audit_views live_drop_pct widget_fail_hits kiosk_evt_hits noc_slo_success_pct sh_render_med severity recommended_action last_seen
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_8_itsi_glass_table_noc_reliability_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -24h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_8_itsi_glass_table_noc_reliability | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector queue time exceeds internal objectives, materialize web and scheduler arms hourly into a summary index keyed by glass_table_id, splunk_server, and fifteen-minute _time, widen operational alerts to earliest=-4h@h on summaries, and retain this search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Splunk Web: open a known NOC glass table on a workstation, record wall-clock time until KPI tiles settle, and compare to render_p95_ms for the same glass_table_id during the matching fifteen-minute bin. Induce a controlled slow search in lab only and confirm render_p95_ms rises within two bins.

Validate scheduler correlation by picking a widget_fail_hits row, opening Job Inspector for the saved search named in widget_id values, and confirming status or zero result_count matches Splunk rows.

Validate splunkd_err rows by tailing splunkd.log on the same splunk_server and searching for the glass table token present in _raw.

Validate audit_views by performing a save-as on a test glass table and confirming _audit volume increases with the expected user.

Validate inventory join coverage by running inputlookup itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv alone; unregistered production tables should appear with display_name unregistered_table until governance catches up.

Validate tstats arm non-empty on estates with Performance acceleration; if empty, repair acceleration before muting the control.

Negative validation: after recovery, when render_p95_ms stays below five seconds, live_drop_pct stays under five percent on wall profiles, widget_fail_hits stays at zero for two hours, and kiosk_evt_hits stays below your baseline, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for canary glass tables across four consecutive schedules.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — P95 render latency above ten seconds on a dense fifty-widget NOC wall: split the glass table into two scenes, convert heavy widgets to pre-accelerated summary indexes, and verify search head CPU during the paint window.

Case 2 — live_drop_pct spikes coincide with reverse-proxy upgrades: increase idle timeouts, confirm WebSocket upgrade headers, and validate stickiness to the same search head member behind the load balancer.

Case 3 — widget_fail_hits cluster after KPI base search edits: roll forward fieldaliases, replay UC-13.2.5 base-search health for the dependent saved searches, and republish the glass table.

Case 4 — splunkd_err shows JavaScript bundle parse failures after an ITSI upgrade: clear browser cache on kiosk hosts, redeploy static assets, and open vendor docs for glass table editor compatibility.

Case 5 — kiosk_evt_hits rise during sev-one bridges: lengthen Splunk Web session timeouts for dedicated TV service accounts, align identity provider idle policies, and disable interactive prompts on the kiosk profile.

Case 6 — dc_viewers near zero for a table labeled is_noc_wall=1: confirm the URL bookmarked on the TV still matches the current glass_table_id after content imports.

Case 7 — audit_views show unexpected editors overnight: rotate credentials, review RBAC grants on the ITSI app, and correlate with deployment automation accounts.

Case 8 — noc_slo_success_pct falls while individual tables look green: eventstats bin alignment may be off; verify is_noc_wall flags and ensure inventory includes every physical wall ID.

Case 9 — Browser autoplay or screen-saver policies freeze the canvas: enforce OS-level keep-awake on NOC PCs and document facilities ownership.

Case 10 — rolling restart of a search head cluster drops live updates without HTTP errors: correlate with captain failover metrics, pause alerts during approved rolling restarts, and validate multisearch arms resume.

Case 11 — Orphan glass tables accumulate high web_hits from automated regression: move automation to staging indexes or tag useragent in lookup to exclude from SLO numerators.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists web access, scheduler widget, splunkd error, audit, and Performance tstats arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, join with inputlookup, case, and tstats appear; final table includes glass_table_id, display_name, splunk_server, owner_team, noc_tier, uri_path, user, useragent, referer, bytes, status, app, widget_id, kpi_id, service_id, render metrics, adoption metrics, SLO context, severity, recommended_action, and last_seen.
visualization: NOC reliability table by glass_table_id and splunk_server with severity coloring, drilldowns to Job Inspector for widget saved searches, splunk_web_access samples, _audit user trails, and inventory owner_team; single-value tiles for noc_slo_success_pct and live_drop_pct; timechart of render_p95_ms with moving average overlay.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS splunk_server
| table splunk_server cpu_load mem_used
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for search head hosts that serve Splunk Web and ITSI Glass Tables; retain thirty days for quarterly NOC display attestations.

knownFalsePositives: Approved content-pack migrations temporarily duplicate glass_table_id rows until bookmarks and inventory reconcile; require change metadata before paging wall teams. Synthetic load tests and screenshot diff bots inflate web_hits without representing human operators; exclude their useragent tokens via lookup. Rolling search head restarts create benign live_drop_pct bursts for one or two bins; pair with captain stability UCs before treating as proxy failure. KPI maintenance freezes raise widget_fail_hits while the canvas remains healthy; correlate with UC-13.2.5 scheduler outcomes. Kiosk browsers that auto-clear cookies nightly mimic authentication churn; document facilities policy before extending session timeouts. Performance datamodel acceleration gaps empty the tstats arm while glass tables still render; repair acceleration rather than muting the entire control. Reverse-proxy health checks that hit glass table URLs without session cookies can skew dc_viewers; segregate probe identities in inventory. Executive drilldowns that open archived glass tables from cold storage snapshots can show orphan hits; gate is_noc_wall flags on current REST exports only. Holiday quiet periods shrink concurrent viewers and can make percentiles noisy on low sample counts; widen tolerance or require minimum web_hits. Finally, manual saves during template edits spike audit_views without security incidents; validate unusual editor accounts against change calendars.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search head clusters running ITSI with Splunk Web access to Glass Tables, typically viewed from Linux or Windows kiosk hosts on large-format NOC displays.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.1 Service Health Score trending, UC-13.2.2 KPI degradation alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode analytics, UC-13.2.5 KPI base search performance ranking in full, UC-13.2.6 rules engine health, UC-13.2.7 predictive health quality, or UC-13.2.18 adaptive threshold drift. Does not remediate network or storage incidents outside Splunk presentation and scheduler planes.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab search head, ingest splunk_web_access rows where uri_path includes /glass_tables/ with response_time_ms above ten thousand milliseconds while scheduler lanes show widget_fail_hits for the same glass_table_id and splunkd_err carries glass table render language; confirm uc_13_2_8_itsi_glass_table_noc_reliability returns critical or high severity within thirty minutes.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when render_p95_ms stays below five seconds for two hours, live_drop_pct stays under five percent, widget_fail_hits is zero, and kiosk_evt_hits is at baseline, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for the canary glass table across four consecutive schedules.

evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_8_itsi_glass_table_noc_reliability with fifteen-minute schedule; exported CSV joined to itsi_glass_table_noc_inventory_slo.csv; screenshots from the Glass Table editor and Service Analyzer for the same glass_table_id.
